rationale: Danaher et al. is cited in multiple functional places: literature framing, hypothesis development, and experimental design justification (urgency/day-of-offer validity). The citing paper explicitly contrasts prior focal-proximity findings with its new competitive locational targeting question, and interprets its focal-location results as consistent with that prior work. This is more than a passing background mention because the cited study helps define what is already known and what the new study adds.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Danaher et al. (2015) as a key prior result on focal-location mobile coupon effectiveness (distance and urgency effects) and then extends that line by testing competitive-location targeting in a randomized field experiment. It positions its contribution as going beyond focal proximity effects to show how targeting near competitors changes response and optimal discount depth.
